Inputs: rootAgentCredentialCommitment, agentTreeRoot, agentMerklePath[20], agentMerkleIndex, delegatorPermissionBitmask, delegateePermissionBitmask, delegateeExpiry, delegatorExpiry, delegationTokenSig, currentTimestamp, optionalGenesisBlockCommitment (optional: Theseus birth-tx hash bound via Poseidon into the root commitment for L1 genesis anchoring). All existing scope-narrowing, expiry-narrowing, liveness, and hop-count constraints are inherited. Proving system: PLONK (no per-circuit ceremony). Expected constraint count: ~23k–25k (within pot16.ptau headroom).

IdentityRegistry.verifyAgentDelegation() — new entry point that accepts (agentRootedDelegationProof, delegatorLeafIndex, delegateeLeafIndex, scopeMask, nonce) and routes through DelegationPlonkVerifier without requiring a paired HumanUniqueness proof. Reuses existing agentTree root history buffer and delegation nullifier mapping. Emits AgentDelegationVerified event for Theseus to index. If the AgentRootedDelegation vkey diverges, deploy a new AgentRootedDelegationPlonkVerifier alongside the existing one.

Complete delegate() stub → delegateAgentToAgent(parentCredential, childCredential, scopeMask, expiry) in @bolyra/sdk v0.3. Internally: validates scopeMask is bitwise subset of parentCredential.permissionBitmask, constructs delegation token with EdDSA signature over (parentCommitment, childCommitment, scopeMask, expiry), calls AgentRootedDelegation prover, returns DelegationResult with proof + chain metadata. Add ScopeEscalationError guard (already defined in error_classes).

Theseus agent registration bridge — maps Theseus L1 agent birth transaction to a Bolyra agentTree leaf. Two paths: (a) lightweight: Theseus orchestrator calls createAgentCredential(theseusModelHash, operatorKey, permissions, expiry) and registers the resulting commitment in IdentityRegistry.agentTree — no cross-chain proof needed, operator signs off-chain; (b) trustless: embed Theseus L1 block header commitment as optionalGenesisBlockCommitment in AgentRootedDelegation inputs, requiring a storage proof relay. Path (a) is the 2-week sprint target. Path (b) is follow-on.

Extend draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01 with Section 7: Agent-Rooted Delegation Chains. Specifies: genesis commitment format, agent-to-agent delegation token schema, chain traversal algorithm, nullifier domain separation from human-rooted chains (prefix byte 0x01 vs 0x00), and conformance test vectors for agent-only chains. Add 8–10 new entries to test-vectors.json.

theseus_integration_surface: Primary integration surface is the IdentityRegistry.verifyAgentDelegation() contract call on Base Sepolia — Theseus's on-chain authorization checks cross-call this function with a PLONK proof, delegator/delegatee leaf indices, required scopeMask, and a per-session nonce. The contract returns a boolean and emits an event; Theseus indexes the event to track authorized sub-agent spawns. Secondary surface is the @bolyra/sdk Node package: Theseus's orchestrator runtime calls delegateAgentToAgent() to generate the PLONK proof client-side before submitting the transaction, avoiding any Bolyra-hosted prover service. For the genesis binding, Theseus passes its agent birth transaction hash to the SDK as optionalGenesisBlockCommitment; in sprint 1 this is operator-attested (off-chain), with a trustless storage-proof path deferred to a follow-on sprint. The did:bolyra DID method and IETF draft provide the identity layer that Theseus can reference in its agent manifest format for interoperability with other orchestrators.

deliverables.name: AgentRootedDelegation design spec
deliverables.description: Circuit I/O table, public signals, trust anchor binding (agentTree Merkle membership at hop 0 via LeanIMT depth 20), genesis-block-commitment optional binding, nullifier design (Poseidon2(agentCredentialCommitment, scope)), hop-count cap 3, expiry-narrowing invariant. One markdown doc Theseus can review before the call.
deliverables.reuses: Delegation.circom design doc + AgentPolicy I/O schema

deliverables.name: AgentRootedDelegation.circom
deliverables.description: Delegation.circom fork: replace humanMerkleRoot anchor at hop 0 with agentTree Merkle membership proof (rootAgentCredentialCommitment, agentMerklePath[20], agentMerkleIndex). Scope-narrowing bitwise subset enforcement, expiry narrowing, identity-bound scope-commitment chain, delegation nullifier replay protection all carried over unchanged. Optional input optionalGenesisBlockCommitment added for Theseus birth-tx binding (Poseidon2 into nullifier domain if nonzero). PLONK only (no per-circuit Groth16 ceremony needed).
deliverables.reuses: Delegation.circom (80% reuse — root anchor is the only structural change)

deliverables.name: Witness generation + fast-mode tests
deliverables.description: Mocha witness-only tests (no full proof) covering: hop-0 agent tree membership, scope cannot expand across hops, expiry must narrow, nullifier uniqueness across chains, genesis commitment binding toggle. Follows existing test:circuits:fast pattern.
deliverables.reuses: circuits/test/ harness + mock agentTree fixtures from AgentPolicy tests

deliverables.name: PLONK trusted setup + vkey generation
deliverables.description: Run snarkjs plonk setup over AgentRootedDelegation.r1cs using pot16.ptau. Generate vkey.json. If constraint count exceeds 2^16, escalate to pot18.ptau — this is the highest-risk step. Export Solidity verifier via snarkjs exportSolidityVerifier.

deliverables.name: verifyAgentDelegation() on IdentityRegistry
deliverables.description: Add a new entry point to IdentityRegistry.sol that routes agent-rooted delegation proofs to the new PlonkVerifier (or reuses existing if vkey-compatible). Accepts: agentTreeRoot (from agentTree root history buffer, 30-entry window), delegationNullifier, scopeCommitment, expiryTimestamp, proof bytes. Stores nullifier on-chain to prevent replay. Does NOT touch the existing verifyHandshake() path.
deliverables.reuses: IdentityRegistry.sol agentTree + nullifier storage + root history buffer

deliverables.name: AgentRootedDelegationPlonkVerifier.sol
deliverables.description: Deploy new verifier contract if vkey diverges from existing DelegationPlonkVerifier. If constraint count and vkey are compatible with existing deployment at 0x28b99c48f19F1680a7D88733ffeb9AFbcB3B95Db, skip this deliverable and wire directly.
deliverables.reuses: snarkjs exportSolidityVerifier output — new artifact but no hand-coding

deliverables.name: SDK: proveAgentDelegation() + verifyAgentDelegation()
deliverables.description: Two new public methods on @bolyra/sdk. proveAgentDelegation(rootAgentCredential, delegateePermissions, delegateeExpiry, agentMerklePath, opts?) generates a PLONK proof via snarkjs/rapidsnark bridge. verifyAgentDelegation(proof, publicSignals, contractAddr) calls the on-chain entry point. Follows existing proveHandshake/verifyHandshake pattern exactly.

deliverables.name: Orchestrator→SubAgent demo script
deliverables.description: Node script: (1) register orchestrator agent in agentTree, (2) orchestrator proves AgentRootedDelegation narrowing FINANCIAL_MEDIUM (bit 3) to FINANCIAL_SMALL (bit 2) for a sub-agent, (3) sub-agent uses delegated credential to call a mock payment endpoint, (4) IdentityRegistry.verifyAgentDelegation() rejects a replay of the same nullifier. Runs against Base Sepolia or a local Hardhat fork. Terminal output only — no UI needed for Sunday.

spec.demo_description: For the Sunday call (2026-06-29): show the live AgentPolicy + existing Delegation circuits running on Base Sepolia as proof of ZKP stack maturity, walk through the AgentRootedDelegation design spec (deliverable 1, ready in 0.5 days), and demonstrate that competitors (Lit/Turnkey/Privy) enforce delegation off-chain while Bolyra enforces it inside the circuit. No working AgentRootedDelegation proof yet — that requires the 10-day sprint. The spec alone is the right Sunday artifact: it shows design credibility without overpromising.
risks: Constraint count exceeds 2^16 (pot16.ptau limit): LeanIMT depth-20 path in AgentRootedDelegation adds ~2K constraints on top of Delegation baseline; if total exceeds 65536, need pot18.ptau (~30min ceremony) and a day of setup re-run.

Theseus EVM compatibility unknown: if Theseus runs a custom VM rather than EVM-compatible execution, Solidity verifier contracts cannot deploy; mitigation is to expose a proof-export API and let Theseus implement their own verifier against the published vkey.json.

Genesis block commitment format unknown: optionalGenesisBlockCommitment binding requires Theseus to expose their birth-tx hash in a Poseidon-hashable format; if they use Keccak256 roots only, the optional input becomes a stub until Theseus ships a Poseidon bridge.
No Theseus testnet access before Sunday: demo on Base Sepolia is the fallback; Theseus integration is spec-level until they provide an RPC endpoint and deployed AgentRegistry contract address.
Solo capacity: circuit + setup + contract + SDK in 10 days is tight with no slack for trusted setup failures; if pot16 ceremony is insufficient, total slips to 12 days.
spec.success_criteria: A terminal run of the orchestrator→subagent demo script completes with: (1) PLONK proof generated in <10s via rapidsnark, (2) verifyAgentDelegation() returns true on-chain for a valid narrowed-scope proof, (3) replay of the same nullifier reverts with 'nullifier already used', (4) an attempted scope-expansion proof (delegateePermissions superset of delegatorPermissions) fails witness generation with a circom constraint error — no on-chain call needed.
spec.theseus_dependencies: RPC endpoint + chain ID for Theseus testnet or devnet; AgentRegistry contract address (or ABI) so Bolyra can read agentTree root for Merkle membership proofs; confirmation that execution environment is EVM-compatible (Solidity verifier deployability); format of agent birth-tx hash if genesis-block-commitment binding is desired at launch; signal on whether they want PLONK (no per-circuit ceremony) or are willing to run Groth16 with a one-time setup.

title: Sybil-resistant agent reputation via ZKP nullifiers for agent marketplaces
description: Agent marketplaces collapse when reputation is cheap to fake — if spinning up a new agent costs nothing, a bad actor resets its reputation every time. Bolyra's HumanUniqueness circuit uses Semaphore v4-style nullifier hashing to prove 'this identity has not been seen before in this context' without revealing which identity it is. Adapting this to agent-rooted trust (where the Theseus genesis transaction replaces the human enrollment) gives each agent a unique, unlinkable nullifier that persists across sessions but cannot be duplicated. This is the foundational primitive for any reputation system on Theseus — Merit Systems' x402scan and Kite both build payment discovery on top of agent reputation, but neither provides Sybil resistance at the identity layer. World's agentkit attempts human-attestation as a proxy for uniqueness but explicitly excludes agent-native scenarios.

rationale.agent_need: The need is well-argued from structural constraints (no human key path, cheap agent spin-up) and confirmed by World agentkit's documented exclusion of agent-native scenarios, but direct Theseus documentation showing they've identified Sybil resistance as a gap is cited only from a context brief, not a primary source.
rationale.zkp_edge: The non-ZKP alternative (stake-weighted reputation as in Fetch.ai) is named and its failure mode (stake can be cheaply recycled, no unlinkability) is structurally argued, though the analysis could more sharply address whether a simpler on-chain commitments scheme could achieve the same without a full ZKP circuit.
rationale.primitive_readiness: Specific circuits (HumanUniqueness fork, AgentPolicy, Delegation), contract functions, and realistic day-estimates are named with clear reuse rationale and no new trusted setup ceremony required, making this one of the better-specified proposals; minor risk is that the theseus-registry-adaptor and marketplace-admission-gate are new contracts requiring audit time not fully accounted for.
rationale.partnership_leverage: The argument that Theseus lacks ZKP circuit expertise is plausible but not evidenced — no team composition data is cited, and Theseus could plausibly adopt Lit Protocol or commission a ZKP team, so the exclusivity of the leverage is moderate at best.

evidence.summary: Theseus is an agent-native L1 with no human-controlled key path, funded by Orange DAO; identity approach is likely raw EdDSA keypairs with no Sybil-resistance layer documented.
x402scan (Merit Systems) and Kite build payment discovery on agent reputation but explicitly lack a Sybil-resistance primitive at the identity layer, confirmed by the opportunity description.
Lit Protocol provides programmable key management and access control for agents but relies on threshold MPC rather than ZKP nullifiers, offering no unlinkable uniqueness guarantee.
Turnkey offers agent wallet infrastructure via secure enclaves but is key-custody focused with no reputation or Sybil-resistance primitive.
Privy handles embedded wallets and agent signing but is human-auth-first; agent-native Sybil resistance is out of scope.
World's agentkit uses human World ID attestation as a proxy for agent uniqueness but explicitly excludes agent-native scenarios, leaving a documented gap Bolyra can fill.
Fetch.ai's Agentverse assigns DIDs to agents but reputation is stake-weighted, not Sybil-resistant at cryptographic level; no ZKP nullifier mechanism present.
SingularityNET uses on-chain agent registry with AGIX staking for reputation; no nullifier-based uniqueness, easy to duplicate with fresh stake.

evidence.source: W3C DID Core + DIF DIDComm v2 (standards)
evidence.summary: DID specs support agent DIDs but provide no uniqueness or Sybil-resistance guarantees; nullifier extension would be non-standard and novel.
Emerging IETF AI agent auth draft focuses on OAuth delegation scopes, not cryptographic uniqueness; ZKP nullifier layer would be additive.

Semaphore v4 is the production-grade ZKP nullifier primitive; Bolyra's HumanUniqueness circuit already reuses its ceremony, making adaptation to agent-rooted trust a circuit-level extension rather than a new trusted setup.
Aztec uses ZKP for account abstraction but not for cross-context Sybil resistance; nullifier model differs from Semaphore's context-scoped design.
Polygon ID deploys ZKP credentials on EVM chains but is credential-presentation focused, not uniqueness/nullifier focused; no agent-native reputation primitive.

mapped_primitives.usage: The Poseidon2(scope, secret) nullifier is the direct Sybil-resistance primitive. For agents, the 'secret' is replaced by a deterministic value derived from the Theseus genesis transaction hash (e.g., Poseidon2(genesisTxHash, operatorSalt)). The scope parameterization means the same agent gets an unlinkable nullifier per marketplace, so reputation is siloed by context. Merkle membership proof (LeanIMT depth 20) handles ~1M registered agents without a new ceremony — reuses the existing agentTree in IdentityRegistry rather than humanTree.

Selective disclosure of the permission bitmask maps directly to reputation tiers — an agent proves 'my reputation tier satisfies requiredScopeMask bits' without revealing the full credential. The identity-bound scope commitment Poseidon3(permissionBitmask, credentialCommitment, expiryTimestamp) can encode a reputation score or tier as the bitmask, with expiry enforcing time-decaying reputation. EdDSA operator signature means a trusted reputation oracle (Theseus validator set or Merit Systems) signs the credential off-chain, and the circuit proves it without on-chain oracle calls.

When a marketplace agent spawns task sub-agents, the delegation chain enforces that the sub-agent's reputation scope is a bitwise subset of the parent's — a low-reputation parent cannot spawn a high-reputation sub-agent. The 3-hop limit and nullifier replay protection on delegation prevent reputation laundering through chain inflation. delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry ensures reputation leases don't outlive the parent credential.

The existing agentTree (LeanIMT with 30-entry root history buffer) is the Merkle accumulator for registered Theseus agents. Atomic handshake verification proves both uniqueness (Groth16 nullifier proof) and reputation level (PLONK AgentPolicy proof) in a single transaction, which is the correct surface for marketplace admission gates. Delegation nullifier replay protection directly prevents the reset-reputation attack described in the opportunity.

The x402 payment integration (integrations/payment-protocols/) provides the monetization rail that reputation gates. An agent with a proven nullifier and reputation tier can unlock x402 paywalls on Theseus-connected services (x402scan, Kite discovery) without deanonymizing itself. The existing integration surface only needs a Bolyra proof check inserted before the payment authorization step.

new_components_needed.description: AgentGenesis circuit: takes Theseus genesis tx hash as the enrollment anchor (replacing human secret in HumanUniqueness). Computes agent identity commitment as Poseidon2(genesisTxHash, operatorSalt) and produces a nullifier Poseidon2(marketplaceScope, genesisCommitment). Needs a storage proof or Merkle inclusion proof that the genesis tx exists on Theseus chain — the non-trivial part is the cross-chain witness. Can fork HumanUniqueness (~16K constraints) with the secret input replaced; storage proof adds ~4-8K constraints depending on trie depth.

TheseusReputationRegistry: deployed on Theseus (or Base Sepolia with Theseus bridge). Stores nullifier→reputationCommitment mappings. Exposes admitAgent(nullifierHash, reputationProof) and revokeAgent(nullifierHash) guarded by the Groth16/PLONK verifiers already deployed. Root history buffer pattern from IdentityRegistry copy-pastes directly. Includes a reputation update path: operator signs new credential, agent proves continuity via nullifier binding without re-enrollment.

Theseus SDK bridge (@bolyra/theseus): thin adapter mapping Theseus chain's agent API to @bolyra/sdk createAgentCredential / proveHandshake. Mirrors the @bolyra/openclaw pattern. Handles genesis tx extraction from Theseus RPC, operatorSalt derivation, and proof submission to TheseusReputationRegistry. Also wraps x402 payment headers with Bolyra proof attestations so x402scan can filter by proven reputation tier.

Reputation claim extension to draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01 and did:bolyra method: defines reputationTier claim type, nullifier binding to genesis tx, and scope encoding for marketplace contexts. Required for Merit Systems / Kite interop so they can verify proofs without coupling to Bolyra internals. Adds ~5 test vectors to the existing conformance suite.

theseus_integration_surface: Four connection points: (1) Verifier contracts — deploy PlonkVerifier and Groth16Verifier (already live on Base Sepolia) to Theseus EVM or bridge via a light-client relay; TheseusReputationRegistry calls into them on-chain, same pattern as IdentityRegistry. (2) SDK bridge — @bolyra/theseus npm package wraps Theseus RPC calls (genesis tx extraction, agentTree leaf construction) and feeds @bolyra/sdk proveHandshake; marketplace operators call one function to get a proof bundle. (3) x402 middleware — insert Bolyra proof verification as an x402 payment authorization hook; x402scan and Kite read the proof attestation from the HTTP 402 challenge response headers rather than building their own Sybil layer. (4) DID resolution — Theseus agents register did:bolyra DIDs that resolve to their agentTree leaf; marketplace UIs resolve DIDs to reputation commitments without touching nullifiers, preserving unlinkability at the presentation layer.

deliverables.name: agent-nullifier-circuit
deliverables.description: Minor fork of HumanUniqueness.circom: replace human secret leaf with Poseidon2(genesisTxHash, operatorSalt), keep LeanIMT depth-20 membership proof and scope-parameterized nullifier hash intact. Reuses pot16.ptau and agentTree in IdentityRegistry — no new trusted setup ceremony needed. Change is ~15 lines of Circom.

deliverables.name: reputation-tier-spec
deliverables.description: Defines how AgentPolicy 8-bit bitmask encodes reputation tiers (bit 0=enrolled, bit 1=verified-payment, bit 2=trusted-small-tx, bit 3=trusted-medium-tx, bits 4-7 reserved for Theseus-native tiers). Specifies EdDSA credential issuance flow: Theseus validator set OR Bolyra-operated oracle signs off-chain, circuit proves tier ≥ threshold without revealing score. Defines expiry convention for time-decaying reputation leases.

deliverables.name: theseus-registry-adaptor
deliverables.description: Thin Solidity contract wrapping IdentityRegistry: accepts (genesisTxHash, operatorSalt, agentLeaf, merkleProof) and inserts agent leaf into agentTree. Enforces one-genesis-one-leaf via a mapping(bytes32 => bool) of spent genesis hashes. Emits AgentEnrolled(nullifierHash, scope). Deploys alongside existing IdentityRegistry on Base Sepolia.

deliverables.name: marketplace-admission-gate
deliverables.description: Solidity contract verifying a (AgentNullifier Groth16 proof + AgentPolicy PLONK proof) pair atomically in a single transaction. Tracks spent nullifiers per scope to block reputation-reset attacks. Exposes admitAgent(nullifierProof, reputationProof, requiredTierMask) → bool. Designed as a composable primitive Theseus marketplace contracts can call as a modifier.

deliverables.name: sdk-theseus-adapter
deliverables.description: TS SDK extension exported from @bolyra/sdk v0.3: theseusGenesisToCredential(genesisTxHash, operatorPrivKey, reputationTier, expiry) → AgentCredential (wraps createAgentCredential with genesis-derived secret). proveTheseusAdmission(credential, scope, requiredMask) calls proveHandshake() against agentTree root. Includes TypeScript types for TheseusGenesisCredential.

deliverables.name: x402-reputation-gate
deliverables.description: Wire Bolyra proof check into existing x402 payment-protocols integration: before x402 authorization executes, require valid (AgentNullifier + minimum reputation tier) proof. Agents below threshold receive HTTP 402 with a Bolyra enrollment URL in WWW-Authenticate header. Connects to x402scan and Kite discovery via their existing payment middleware hooks.

deliverables.name: theseus-sybil-demo
deliverables.description: Single Node script showing the full attack-and-defense loop on Base Sepolia: (1) enroll agent keyed to a real Theseus genesis tx hash, (2) issue reputation credential at tier 2 via oracle, (3) prove admission to mock marketplace gate contract, (4) attempt second enrollment with same genesis hash → reverted. Console output shows nullifier hash, proof time, and block explorer link. Runs in under 2 minutes end-to-end.

deliverables.name: theseus-integration-spec
deliverables.description: Technical doc for Theseus team covering: genesis tx hash derivation convention and salt spec, contract ABI and deployment addresses on Base Sepolia, proof flow diagram (enroll → oracle-sign → prove → gate), oracle key custody options (Theseus validator multisig vs Bolyra-operated), EVM compatibility checklist, and LeanIMT depth-20 capacity analysis (~1M agents).

spec.demo_description: For the Sunday call (tomorrow): show the existing AgentPolicy circuit live — prove a reputation tier from an EdDSA-signed credential in ~50ms via snarkjs, no server needed. Then open HumanUniqueness.circom and show the 15-line diff that swaps the human secret for Poseidon2(genesisTxHash, operatorSalt) — making the circuit change feel trivially concrete. Walk through the integration spec doc showing the full proof flow diagram. Key talking point: the Sybil-resistance primitive already works, the Theseus integration is a 2-week engineering task not a research problem. Ask Theseus for genesis tx format spec and EVM compatibility confirmation to unblock day-1 work.
risks: Theseus is not EVM-compatible: Solidity verifier contracts cannot deploy; would require porting Groth16/PLONK verifiers to their VM or moving verification off-chain with on-chain commitment anchoring — adds 2-3 weeks.

Genesis tx hash is not stable pre-finality or not accessible to the agent proving environment: need a trusted relay mechanism, which reintroduces a centralization point the ZKP was supposed to eliminate.

Reputation oracle key custody unresolved: EdDSA signing key needs a clear owner (Theseus validator set, Bolyra-operated oracle, or multi-sig) before production; wrong choice here becomes the system's trust bottleneck.

agentTree root history buffer (30 entries) saturates under rapid Theseus agent registration bursts, causing membership proof failures for agents enrolled before the buffer rolled over — may need to extend buffer or add an archival root checkpoint.
World agentkit or Lit Protocol ship a competing nullifier primitive in the next 90 days given the documented gap; speed of demo to Theseus matters more than polish.
spec.success_criteria: A single Base Sepolia transaction: registers a Theseus agent keyed to a real genesis tx hash, verifies a Groth16 nullifier proof that no prior enrollment exists for that hash in that marketplace scope, verifies a PLONK AgentPolicy proof that reputation tier >= required threshold, and rejects a second registration attempt with the same genesis hash — all with end-to-end proving time under 500ms on commodity hardware and gas under 300k.
spec.theseus_dependencies: EVM compatibility confirmation or custom VM ABI if not EVM; genesis transaction hash derivation spec (algorithm, salt convention, finality timing); testnet RPC endpoint and faucet (Base Sepolia or Theseus devnet); decision on whether Theseus validator set will operate the EdDSA reputation oracle or Bolyra runs one independently; approximate target agent count at launch to validate LeanIMT depth-20 capacity of ~1M agents.

title: ZKP-enforced x402 spending limits for autonomous Theseus agents
description: x402 is now an industry standard (Linux Foundation, Coinbase, AWS Bedrock, Stripe) and Bolyra already ships @bolyra/payment-protocols implementing x402 flows. The AgentPolicy circuit's permission bits 2 (FINANCIAL_SMALL <$100), 3 (FINANCIAL_MEDIUM <$10K), and 4 (FINANCIAL_UNLIMITED) provide ZKP-enforced spending authorization: an agent proves it holds a credential with the required financial permission bit set, without revealing its full credential or who granted it. For Theseus, this means autonomous agents can make x402 payments with cryptographically enforced spending caps — no human approval needed, no trusted intermediary. The FINANCIAL_MEDIUM implication of FINANCIAL_SMALL (cumulative bit encoding, enforced in AgentPolicy circuit) prevents privilege escalation. Integration path: @bolyra/payment-protocols + existing AgentPolicy circuit + a thin Theseus-native SDK wrapper calling proveHandshake() before each x402 payment request.

rationale.agent_need: x402 multi-vendor adoption (Linux Foundation, AWS Bedrock, Coinbase) is well-evidenced, but the Theseus-specific 'no human key path' constraint comes from an unconfirmed source (theseuschain.com blocked), leaving the core premise — that Theseus agents specifically need ZKP spending caps — as a structural inference rather than confirmed evidence.
rationale.zkp_edge: Non-ZKP alternatives are named and their failure modes are structurally argued (Coinbase custodial, Lit MPC, Turnkey server-side), and the in-circuit cumulative-bit enforcement preventing privilege escalation is a genuine structural advantage, but the proposal underweights that custodial spending limits (Coinbase Agentic Wallets) already solve the practical problem for most enterprise use cases without ZKP complexity.
rationale.primitive_readiness: Specific circuits (AgentPolicy PLONK, Delegation), SDK methods (createAgentCredential, proveHandshake), and contracts (IdentityRegistry, PlonkVerifier) are named; new work is scoped to proveAgentPayment() (2d), x402PaymentMiddleware (2d), and PaymentAuthorizer.sol (3d), with @bolyra/payment-protocols already shipping x402 support — realistic and well-decomposed.
rationale.partnership_leverage: The case for why Theseus cannot build this themselves is entirely missing — no assessment of Theseus's team ZKP proficiency, no evidence they lack an identity solution in progress, and credible drop-in competitors (Lit Protocol, Turnkey, Coinbase Agentic Wallets) are available; the partnership value proposition for Theseus is asserted but not demonstrated.

evidence.source: theseuschain.com (not confirmed live — web search blocked)
evidence.summary: Theseus describes 'Agency for Agents' with agent-held keys and no human key path, implying raw EdDSA keypairs with no ZKP authorization layer — the exact gap Bolyra fills.

evidence.source: x402.org — Linux Foundation / Coinbase / AWS Bedrock / Stripe adoption
evidence.summary: x402 has achieved multi-vendor endorsement as the HTTP-native payment standard for autonomous agents, making ZKP-enforced spending caps on x402 flows an immediately deployable primitive.
AWS Bedrock explicitly frames x402 as the payment layer for agentic commerce, validating that enterprise agent stacks will consume x402 payment flows at scale.
Coinbase Agentic Wallets launch signals that custodial spending-limit enforcement is the incumbent solution — ZKP-native enforcement via Bolyra AgentPolicy circuit is the trustless alternative.

evidence.source: Lit Protocol — PKP (Programmable Key Pairs) for agents
evidence.summary: Lit offers distributed key management for agents but relies on MPC threshold signing, not ZKP authorization proofs — no cryptographically enforced permission-bit spending caps.
Turnkey provides policy-based signing with server-side enforcement; no on-chain ZKP proof of authorization — spending limits are custodial, not cryptographic.
Privy targets consumer-facing embedded wallets; agent use cases are secondary and lack any delegation or spending-cap primitives.
Both Fetch.ai and SingularityNET have agent-to-agent payment primitives but no ZKP-enforced permission scoping; authorization is contract-level ACL, not cryptographic proof.

evidence.source: W3C Verifiable Credentials + DID spec (did:bolyra method in spec/)
evidence.summary: VC/DID standards provide the presentation layer but have no native spending-cap enforcement; Bolyra's AgentPolicy circuit adds the missing cryptographic enforcement layer.
Bolyra already ships x402 flow integration with AgentPolicy circuit wiring; the Theseus integration is a thin SDK wrapper on existing primitives, not greenfield.

evidence.source: Orange DAO / YC alumni DAO — $100K seed for Theseus (2025)
evidence.summary: Early-stage funding means Theseus is pre-mainnet, likely still architecting identity — optimal timing to influence their auth stack before it ossifies.

mapped_primitives.usage: Core authorization primitive. Permission bits 2/3/4 (FINANCIAL_SMALL/MEDIUM/UNLIMITED) map directly to x402 payment tiers. Selective disclosure proves requiredScopeMask bits are set without leaking full credential or operator identity — exactly what x402 payment endpoints need to gate access. Cumulative-bit enforcement in-circuit prevents a FINANCIAL_SMALL credential from claiming FINANCIAL_MEDIUM rights.

Enables the autonomous-agent pattern without keeping the human online per payment. Human enrolls once, delegates FINANCIAL_SMALL or FINANCIAL_MEDIUM scope to a Theseus agent key. Scope narrowing (delegateeScope ⊆ delegatorScope) enforces the spending cap in-circuit. Expiry narrowing prevents delegated credentials from outliving the parent. Delegation nullifier on IdentityRegistry prevents replay of the same delegation token.

On-chain proof verification anchor. Root history buffers (30 entries, humanTree + agentTree) allow stale-root tolerance across the 12-second Base block time without requiring synchronous Merkle root updates per payment. Delegation chain-state tracking enforces the 3-hop maximum for nested Theseus sub-agents.

@bolyra/payment-protocols already implements x402 request/response flow. It becomes the transport layer: intercepts the 402 challenge, triggers proveHandshake() or a standalone AgentPolicy proof, attaches the proof as a Bearer token or custom x402 header field before retrying the request.

SDK entry points for credential issuance (operator keys in Theseus operator infra) and proof generation at payment time. proveHandshake() currently requires both humanProof and agentProof — for fully autonomous payments where no human is online, a standalone AgentPolicy prove path is needed (see new_components_needed).

Indirect: used once at enrollment to establish the human Merkle leaf that anchors subsequent Delegation grants. Not invoked per-payment in the autonomous flow — the delegation chain carries forward the human binding without requiring the human to be online.

new_components_needed.description: PaymentAuthorizer: a lightweight IdentityRegistry extension (or standalone verifier) that accepts a single PlonkVerifier call against an AgentPolicy proof (or DelegationPlonkVerifier for delegated agents) without requiring a simultaneous HumanUniqueness Groth16 proof. Current verifyHandshake() is atomic human+agent; x402 payment flows are agent-only at call time. Emits a PaymentAuthorized(nullifier, scopeMask, expiryTimestamp) event for off-chain audit.

provePayment(agentCredential, requiredPermission, paymentNonce) → PaymentProof: thin SDK function wrapping AgentPolicy witness generation + snarkjs prove for the payment-only path. Distinct from proveHandshake() — no HumanUniqueness witness required. Attaches proof to x402 402-Payment header as a base64-encoded protobuf or JSON envelope per @bolyra/payment-protocols wire format.

theseus-bolyra adapter: a Theseus-native SDK wrapper (~200 LOC) that hooks into Theseus's HTTP client layer. Intercepts 402 responses, calls provePayment(), retries with proof attached. Reads agent credentials from Theseus's existing secret store. Exposes a SpendingPolicy config (maxTierPerDomain, requireDelegation: bool) so operators can pin individual agents to FINANCIAL_SMALL even if the credential allows FINANCIAL_MEDIUM.

x402-bolyra-proof-envelope spec: a one-page extension to draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01 defining how the AgentPolicy/Delegation proof is encoded in the x402 Payment header field — field names, proof format version, scope mask encoding, nullifier for idempotency. Needed so x402 endpoints can verify without knowing Bolyra internals.

theseus_integration_surface: Three-layer surface: (1) Contract layer — Theseus's payment infrastructure calls PaymentAuthorizer.verifyPaymentProof(proof, requiredScopeMask, nonce) on Base Sepolia (or mainnet fork) to gate each x402 payment on-chain; the contract emits PaymentAuthorized events Theseus can index. (2) SDK bridge — theseus-bolyra adapter wraps @bolyra/sdk and @bolyra/payment-protocols; Theseus operators call initBolyraPayments(agentCredential, policy) once at agent startup, after which the adapter is transparent. (3) Operator key management — Theseus operators issue AgentCredentials via createAgentCredential(modelHash, operatorKey, [FINANCIAL_MEDIUM], expiry), stored in their existing secret management; the SDK bridge reads them at prove time without storing proof artifacts.

deliverables.name: proveAgentPayment() — standalone agent proof path
deliverables.description: New SDK export that generates an AgentPolicy PLONK proof for a single payment authorization without requiring the human to be online. Accepts (delegatedCredential, requiredPermissionBit, paymentAmountCents, sessionNonce) and returns a proof + public signals. proveHandshake() currently requires both human+agent — this headless variant is the critical missing piece for fully autonomous Theseus agents.
deliverables.reuses: AgentPolicy circuit (unchanged), createAgentCredential, existing snarkjs PLONK prover

deliverables.name: x402PaymentMiddleware — proof-gated x402 transport
deliverables.description: Thin middleware wrapping @bolyra/payment-protocols. Intercepts the 402 challenge, inspects the required payment tier, calls proveAgentPayment() with the matching FINANCIAL_* bit, attaches the base64-encoded proof as x-bolyra-proof header (or Bearer token per x402 spec), then retries the request. Handles the FINANCIAL_SMALL / MEDIUM / UNLIMITED tier routing from the 402 WWW-Authenticate header.

deliverables.name: PaymentAuthorizer.sol — on-chain spending-tier verifier
deliverables.description: Lightweight Solidity contract (150-200 lines). Accepts an AgentPolicy PLONK proof + public signals, calls the existing PlonkVerifier, checks that requiredScopeMask matches the requested payment tier (FINANCIAL_SMALL=0x04, MEDIUM=0x08, UNLIMITED=0x10), enforces cumulative-bit implication (MEDIUM implies SMALL), checks nullifier against IdentityRegistry to prevent replay, emits PaymentAuthorized(agentNullifier, tier, nonce). x402 payment endpoint calls this before releasing the resource. Deployed to Base Sepolia.
deliverables.reuses: Existing PlonkVerifier (AgentPolicy), IdentityRegistry nullifier store, Hardhat test suite

deliverables.name: @bolyra/theseus — Theseus agent runtime wrapper
deliverables.description: Tiny npm package (~200 lines) wrapping @bolyra/sdk with Theseus-idiomatic ergonomics: TheseusAgent class with issueCredential(eddsaPrivKey, permissions, expiry), payWithProof(url, amount), and delegateTo(subAgentPubKey, narrowedPermissions). Designed for Theseus agent runtime environment — assumes EdDSA keys are already held by the agent, no browser/wallet dependency.

deliverables.name: End-to-end demo script
deliverables.description: Node.js script that runs the full flow headlessly: (1) operator issues AgentPolicy credential with FINANCIAL_SMALL (bit 2) to a Theseus agent EdDSA key, (2) agent encounters a mock x402 endpoint requiring $50 payment, (3) middleware generates PLONK proof in <8s via snarkjs, (4) PaymentAuthorizer.sol verifies on Base Sepolia, (5) payment succeeds, (6) replay of same nonce fails. Script outputs proof size, proving time, and gas cost. Runnable in <2 minutes from npm install.
deliverables.reuses: All above components, existing Base Sepolia deploy infrastructure

deliverables.name: Theseus integration spec (one-pager)
deliverables.description: Markdown doc covering: agent key format assumptions (EdDSA on Baby Jubjub), PaymentAuthorizer ABI, x-bolyra-proof header format, proof verification gas benchmarks, delegation chain setup for sub-agents, and what Theseus must expose (operator registration endpoint or on-chain). Written for Theseus eng team, not general audience.

spec.demo_description: For the Sunday June 29 call: show the live AgentPolicy circuit financial permission bits in circuits/src/AgentPolicy.circom, walk through @bolyra/payment-protocols x402 flow with a mock credential, and share a 90-second architecture diagram showing how proveAgentPayment() slots into Theseus agent runtime. No live proof on Sunday — snarkjs proving takes ~6s and Base Sepolia tx needs setup time. The narrative is: 'here is every primitive already built, here is the 2-week plan to wire them.' The demo script will be the artifact for the follow-up call.
risks: Theseus VM may not be EVM-compatible — PaymentAuthorizer.sol assumes EVM; if Theseus runs a custom VM, the on-chain verifier needs a rewrite that could add 1-2 weeks
No Theseus testnet access before Sunday call — integration is conceptual until Eric Wang provides endpoint or chain docs; all solo work must be on Base Sepolia until then

proveHandshake() assumes Baby Jubjub EdDSA (same curve as Circom/snarkjs) — Theseus agent keys may use a different curve (secp256k1 is common); curve mismatch would require AgentPolicy circuit modification (1-2 day fix but a real risk)
Solo founder bandwidth: 10 dev-days assumes focused blocks; any Theseus back-and-forth async latency or other product fires will push the timeline
x402 header spec is still evolving — the proof attachment mechanism (Bearer vs custom header) may need adjustment if Theseus's x402 implementation diverges from x402.org reference
spec.success_criteria: End-to-end demo script runs clean: agent credential issued, x402 payment authorized via on-chain PLONK proof verification, replay rejected, total e2e latency under 15s, gas under 300k on Base Sepolia. Theseus eng team can npm install @bolyra/theseus and run the demo with their own EdDSA keys.
spec.theseus_dependencies: Need from Theseus before integration can go beyond Base Sepolia mock: (1) EVM compatibility confirmation or custom VM bytecode format, (2) agent EdDSA key curve (Baby Jubjub assumed — confirm), (3) testnet RPC endpoint + faucet access, (4) how agents currently identify themselves on-chain (raw pubkey? DID? address?), (5) whether Theseus has an operator registry concept or if Bolyra must deploy IdentityRegistry on Theseus chain. Without (1) and (2), the Base Sepolia demo is a full proof of concept.

title: ZKP Agent Birth Certificate via PLONK (No Per-Agent Trusted Setup)
description: When a new agent spawns on Theseus, it needs a verifiable identity commitment that (a) doesn't require a human ceremony, (b) proves the agent was legitimately born from the L1 (not fabricated off-chain), and (c) is portable to other chains. Bolyra's PLONK-based AgentPolicy circuit avoids per-agent trusted setup — the universal SRS is the ceremony. An agent can generate its credential commitment at spawn time, submit a PLONK proof to the IdentityRegistry, and receive a DID (did:bolyra:...) anchored to the Theseus genesis block. This directly addresses the 'no human key path' constraint: the L1 itself is the root of trust, and ZKP is what proves legitimate birth without a human CA. Bolyra already ships PLONK zkeys for AgentPolicy — this is a configuration extension, not a new circuit.

rationale.agent_need: Theseus documentation is not publicly accessible so evidence rests on structural inference from 'no human key path' plus weak analogies from Fetch.ai/SingularityNET that use conventional PKI, not ZKP birth certs.
rationale.zkp_edge: The proposal names Lit Protocol (MPC-based) and Turnkey (TEE-based) as non-ZKP alternatives and correctly identifies that neither produces portable, chain-anchored ZKP credential commitments, but the failure mode argument is asserted rather than demonstrated with a concrete attack or limitation scenario.
rationale.primitive_readiness: Existing PLONK zkeys, PlonkVerifier, IdentityRegistry, and createAgentCredential() are all directly reusable; new work is a thin SDK wrapper (~0.5d), one orchestration contract (~1d), a spec addendum (~0.5d), and a demo — well-scoped and realistic.
rationale.partnership_leverage: The argument that Theseus cannot build this in <3 months is plausible given Circom/PLONK expertise requirements, but the proposal doesn't assess whether Theseus has a crypto team, whether they are already evaluating Lit/Turnkey, or why they would prefer Bolyra over a custodial TEE solution that ships faster.

evidence.source: theseuschain.com (not reachable via search in this session)
evidence.summary: Theseus is an agent-native L1 founded 2025, funded by Orange DAO; no public documentation of their identity/auth primitive was accessible — likely raw EdDSA keypairs at spawn time with no ZKP layer.

Lit Protocol offers programmable key management via threshold MPC for agents and dApps, but relies on a human-operated node network and does NOT produce ZKP-verifiable credential commitments — no PLONK or Groth16 proofs anchored to chain genesis.

Turnkey provides TEE-backed key custody for agents (used by Coinbase Agentic Wallets per signal URL), but custody is custodial/TEE-attested rather than ZKP-proven, and does not produce portable DID documents or on-chain credential registries.
Privy targets human-facing embedded wallets, has begun supporting server-side agent wallets, but assumes a human operator root of trust — not suited for Theseus's 'no human key path' constraint.

evidence.source: W3C DID Core Spec / DIF DIDComm v2 / IETF draft-klrc-aiagent
evidence.summary: No ratified standard for agent-to-agent ZKP-backed DID authentication exists as of mid-2026; DIF and IETF working groups have drafts but none address agent-native L1 genesis anchoring or PLONK-based birth certificates.

Fetch.ai agents carry on-chain identities (agent addresses) but use conventional PKI signing — no ZKP birth certificate, no universal SRS, and delegation is handled via smart contract logic rather than circuit-enforced scope narrowing.
SingularityNET's agent registry is metadata-only (IPFS CIDs + on-chain hashes); no cryptographic proof-of-legitimate-spawn and no delegation circuit.

evidence.source: AWS blog: x402 and Agentic Commerce (signal URL)
evidence.summary: x402 HTTP payment protocol for agents relies on wallet-signed requests — highlights demand for agent-native credential primitives but does not provide identity birth certificates or ZKP proofs.

Coinbase's agentic wallet product uses Turnkey TEE custody — confirms strong market demand for agent key management at spawn time but remains TEE/MPC rather than ZKP, leaving the 'portable, no-ceremony, L1-anchored proof' gap open.

zkLogin proves OAuth identity via Groth16 on Sui — closest deployed ZKP identity primitive, but human-rooted (OAuth) and chain-specific; does not generalize to agent-spawn proofs on arbitrary EVM-compatible L1s.

Semaphore provides ZKP group membership with nullifiers (Bolyra reuses its ceremony for HumanUniqueness), but is explicitly human-biometric-rooted — cannot serve as agent birth certificate without stripping the biometric assumption.

evidence.source: Bolyra CLAUDE.md / AgentPolicy PLONK zkeys (local context)
evidence.summary: Bolyra already ships PLONK zkeys for AgentPolicy with universal SRS (pot16.ptau), 8-bit cumulative permission encoding, and a DID method (did:bolyra:) — the primitives for an agent birth certificate without per-agent ceremony are already built.

mapped_primitives.usage: Core birth-certificate primitive. Agent generates credentialCommitment at spawn time using createAgentCredential(). PLONK universal SRS IS the ceremony — no per-agent trusted setup. The operatorKey role is filled by Theseus L1's own signing authority (sequencer/validator key or a deterministic key derived from the genesis block hash), making L1 the root of trust without a human CA. Selective disclosure of permission bitmask lets the agent prove capability subset to any relying party without revealing full policy.

agentTree (LeanIMT, depth 20) registers the agent's leaf after PLONK proof verification. Root history buffer (30 entries) provides stale-proof tolerance during chain reorgs. Nonce equality and delegation nullifier replay protection guard against duplicate registrations across forks. For Theseus-native deployment the contract needs redeployment on Theseus's EVM (same bytecode, new address); Base Sepolia can serve as the canonical registry with a cross-chain relay if Theseus is EVM-compatible.

Already deployed at 0x28b99c48f19F1680a7D88733ffeb9AFbcB3B95Db on Base Sepolia. The same vkey.json can generate a verifier contract deployable on Theseus's chain with zero circuit changes. Self-contained PLONK proof portability means the proof bundle (pi_a, pi_b, pi_c + public inputs) can be verified by any chain holding the verification key — cross-chain portability is inherent.

Enables Theseus sub-agent spawning with enforced scope narrowing. A parent agent's birth certificate becomes the delegator credential; child agents receive scope-narrowed delegatee credentials with delegateeExpiry <= delegatorExpiry. Three-hop max (human -> orchestrator agent -> task agent -> sub-agent) maps cleanly to Theseus's agent hierarchy. Delegator key binding (UC3.1 fix) ensures the birth certificate chain is unforgeable.

DID anchored to Theseus genesis block: did:bolyra:agent:<agentId> where agentId = Poseidon(genesisBlockHash, credentialCommitment). The DID document carries the PLONK proof reference, agentTree Merkle path, and chain-of-origin metadata. Portable resolution — any resolver with the verification key can validate without contacting Theseus's chain directly. Minor extension to the DID method spec to include genesisAnchor as a first-class DID document field.

createAgentCredential(modelHash, operatorKey, permissions, expiry) is the direct entry point. modelHash encodes the agent's code digest; operatorKey is Theseus L1's signing key (or a key derived from genesis hash via a deterministic KDF). No new SDK function needed — a genesisAnchor option parameter added to the options object satisfies the L1-birth provenance requirement without breaking the existing API.

The mutual ZKP auth handshake (proveHandshake + verifyHandshake) becomes the Theseus agent-to-agent trust handshake. When two Theseus agents interact, both present birth-certificate-backed proofs; the IETF draft's nonce binding prevents replay across sessions. This gives Theseus a standards-track wire format instead of a custom auth protocol.

new_components_needed.description: genesis-anchor convention: define how Theseus genesis block hash is incorporated into credentialCommitment — specifically, a Poseidon3(modelHash, genesisBlockHash, operatorPubKeyHash) preimage convention so L1 provenance is cryptographically bound. Extend did:bolyra method spec to add genesisAnchor as a first-class field in the DID document and add 3–5 test vectors to test-vectors.json.

Add optional genesisAnchor parameter to createAgentCredential options and incorporate it into the credential commitment hash. Expose a deriveOperatorKeyFromGenesis(genesisBlockHash) helper using Baby Jubjub deterministic scalar derivation so Theseus L1 can act as operator without a human-generated key. Backwards-compatible — existing callers unaffected.

theseus-bolyra-spawn adapter: a lightweight TS module that hooks into Theseus's agent spawn lifecycle, calls createAgentCredential at birth, submits the PLONK proof to IdentityRegistry (or Theseus-native verifier), and returns a did:bolyra DID to the agent runtime. Pattern matches langchain-bolyra-tool stub already in integrations/.

Deploy PlonkVerifier + IdentityRegistry on Theseus's EVM (if EVM-compatible). No Solidity changes needed — same bytecode, new deployment script targeting Theseus RPC. If Theseus is not EVM-compatible, a thin verification relay contract on Base Sepolia with cross-chain message passing (e.g. via L1 state proof) is the fallback.

theseus_integration_surface: Three connection points: (1) SDK bridge — Theseus agent runtime imports @bolyra/sdk, calls createAgentCredential() at spawn with genesis-anchored operatorKey, receives a PLONK proof bundle and did:bolyra DID; (2) Verifier contract — PlonkVerifier + IdentityRegistry deployed on Theseus's EVM chain (or Base Sepolia as canonical registry with Theseus relaying proofs via L1 state root); (3) DID resolution — Theseus's agent directory resolves did:bolyra DIDs against the agentTree Merkle root, enabling any other Theseus agent or external service to verify birth provenance without trusting Theseus's own assertions. The mutual ZKP handshake (draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01) then serves as the agent-to-agent trust wire protocol, giving Theseus a standards-track inter-agent auth layer on top of the birth certificate foundation.

deliverables.name: createTheseusAgentCredential()
deliverables.description: Thin wrapper (~50 LOC) around existing createAgentCredential() that derives operatorKey deterministically from Poseidon(genesisBlockHash, agentSeed) so the L1 genesis block is the root of trust with zero human CA involvement. Exported from @bolyra/sdk as a named export alongside existing API.

deliverables.name: TheseusBirthCertRegistrar.sol
deliverables.description: Minimal Solidity contract deployable on Base Sepolia (or any EVM): accepts PLONK proof + genesisBlockHash as public input, delegates to existing PlonkVerifier (0x28b99c48f19F1680a7D88733ffeb9AFbcB3B95Db), inserts credentialCommitment into agentTree, emits AgentRegistered(did, credentialCommitment, genesisAnchor). No new verifier logic — pure orchestration over existing deployed verifier.
deliverables.reuses: IdentityRegistry.sol agentTree logic + deployed PlonkVerifier on Base Sepolia

deliverables.name: did:bolyra genesisAnchor extension
deliverables.description: Two-paragraph addendum to spec/did-method-bolyra.md: defines agentId = Poseidon(genesisBlockHash, credentialCommitment), DID = did:bolyra:agent:<agentId>, and genesisAnchor as a first-class DID document field carrying chain-of-origin metadata. Pure spec text, no new crypto, no circuit changes.

deliverables.name: theseus-birth-cert-demo.ts
deliverables.description: Self-contained runnable script: (1) takes mock genesisBlockHash (keccak256('theseus-genesis-v0') until real testnet), (2) calls createTheseusAgentCredential(), (3) loads pre-generated PLONK proof artifact from disk to avoid 30s live wait, (4) submits to TheseusBirthCertRegistrar on Base Sepolia, (5) prints DID + Merkle inclusion path + Etherscan link. End-to-end runtime under 5 seconds.

spec.demo_description: Live terminal demo: agent seed + mock genesis hash in → PLONK proof verified (pre-generated, sub-second) → DID printed: did:bolyra:agent:<hash>. Etherscan link showing AgentRegistered event on Base Sepolia with credentialCommitment and genesisAnchor visible. One comparison slide: Lit Protocol = MPC custody (node network, no ZKP), Turnkey = TEE attestation (custodial, no portable DID), Privy = human root required. Bolyra = ZKP-verifiable birth cert, no human CA, PLONK universal SRS IS the ceremony, portable to any EVM chain. Closing line: 'The Theseus genesis block replaces the human ceremony. The L1 is the trust root.'
risks: Theseus is not EVM-compatible: TheseusBirthCertRegistrar cannot deploy natively — mitigate by positioning Base Sepolia as canonical registry with a cross-chain relay stub; proof portability is inherent (any chain holding vkey.json can verify)
PLONK proof generation takes ~30s with snarkjs live — mitigate by pre-generating proof artifact before the call and loading from file; demo still shows real on-chain submission
Theseus genesis block hash unavailable (no testnet access before Sunday) — use keccak256('theseus-genesis-v0') as deterministic mock; swap in real value the moment testnet RPC is shared

Theseus may already be committed to TEE or MPC solution — frame Bolyra as additive ZKP attestation layer on top of their existing key custody, not a replacement; TEE + ZKP is stronger than either alone
Solo founder bandwidth: 2.5 days of work must land by Saturday EOD — if time-pressed, cut contract deploy and show local proof verification only; the cryptography story holds without the on-chain tx
spec.success_criteria: Demo runs end-to-end without errors from a cold clone: agent seed + genesis hash in, PLONK proof verified, DID printed, AgentRegistered event visible on Base Sepolia Etherscan. The integration is working when Eric Wang asks 'how do we wire this into our agent spawn flow on mainnet?' — that question is the buying signal.
spec.theseus_dependencies: Nice-to-have before Sunday, none are blockers: (1) Theseus testnet RPC endpoint to pull real genesis block hash — mock substitutes cleanly. (2) Confirmation of EVM compatibility for native TheseusBirthCertRegistrar deploy — Base Sepolia serves as canonical registry in the interim. (3) Their current agent spawn API/spec so operatorKey derivation path matches their actual key generation — determines whether Poseidon or keccak256 derivation is the right join point. All three can be collected on the Sunday call and wired in as a follow-up PR.

title: ZKP-Enforced Delegation Chain with Scope Monotonicity on Theseus L1
description: On Theseus, Agent A delegates to Agent B which delegates to Agent C — without circuit-level enforcement, Agent C can claim permissions Agent A never granted (privilege escalation). Bolyra's Delegation circuit enforces one-way scope narrowing at the proof level: a delegatee cannot construct a valid proof claiming bits the delegator lacked. This is structurally unsolvable with ACLs alone on an agent-native L1 because there is no human to audit the chain in real time. The 8-bit cumulative encoding (bits 2→3→4 implication) is already implemented and the circuit is live on Base Sepolia — adapting it for agent-rooted trust (no human at root) is the key extension.

rationale.agent_need: Strong structural argument from 'no human auditor' constraint plus Fetch.ai analog showing multi-hop privilege escalation emerged in the only comparable deployed agent L1, but zero direct citation from Theseus docs or codebase (web search unavailable) leaves a required evidence gap.
rationale.zkp_edge: Concretely names Lit Protocol (ACL/MPC), Turnkey (off-chain policy), and ACL smart contracts as alternatives, then identifies a structural failure for each — none can cryptographically bind scope narrowing across delegation hops without a trusted third-party auditor, which is a real structural gap not a 'ZKP is cooler' argument.
rationale.primitive_readiness: Correctly maps AgentPolicy (no change), DelegationPlonkVerifier (minor redeployment), and IdentityRegistry (major), but the critical AgentRootedDelegation.circom requires rewriting the trust-root input wiring — a new circuit that the spec itself estimates at 5 days, underweighting that any circuit change triggers new trusted setup, integration testing, and verifier contract regeneration adding 3–5 more days.
rationale.partnership_leverage: Correctly identifies the ZKP circuit design and Circom proficiency gap that Fetch.ai and SingularityNET never closed, but provides no direct evidence on Theseus's team crypto background, no confirmation they lack an identity solution in progress, and does not address the risk that they adopt Lit Protocol (which is production-ready today) as the path of least resistance.

evidence.summary: Theseus is an agent-native L1 with no human key path, $100K Orange DAO seed, call booked 2026-06-29 — identity/auth approach not yet publicly documented.
Could not retrieve Theseus GitHub repos, docs, or community discussions; live search required to fill this gap before Sunday's call.
Lit provides programmable MPC key management and conditional signing for agents, but does not enforce delegation scope monotonicity at the circuit level — ACL-based, not ZKP-enforced.
Turnkey offers embedded wallet infrastructure with policy engine for agent signing, but policies are off-chain and not cryptographically binding across delegation hops.
Privy targets human-facing embedded wallets; lacks native agent-to-agent delegation model and has no ZKP layer.
Emerging IETF draft on AI agent identity; no finalized spec for agent-to-agent delegation chain integrity or ZKP-enforced scope monotonicity.
Fetch.ai has an agent framework (AEA) with DID-based identity but relies on traditional PKI and ACLs — no ZKP delegation enforcement, privilege escalation risk across multi-hop agent chains.
AI marketplace on blockchain; agent identity is marketplace-account-level, no structured delegation primitive or scope narrowing.

Semaphore v4 ceremony (depth 20) is already reused by Bolyra's HumanUniqueness circuit — closest deployed ZKP identity system, but human-rooted; no agent-rooted trust chain equivalent exists in production.
ZKP-native L2 with privacy-preserving account abstraction; not agent-native, no structured permission bit model — different design point from Bolyra's cumulative-bit encoding.
Bolyra's Delegation circuit with 8-bit cumulative encoding is live on Base Sepolia — only known ZKP system enforcing scope monotonicity across multi-hop agent delegation chains.

mapped_primitives.usage: Core enforcement primitive. The PLONK Delegation circuit already implements scope narrowing (delegateeScope ⊆ delegatorScope), expiry narrowing, max-3-hop enforcement, and identity-bound scope commitment chain linking. For Theseus, each hop in an A→B→C chain submits a Delegation proof; the circuit guarantee that C cannot claim bits A never granted is already cryptographically enforced — no ACL auditor needed.

Establishes the root credential at hop 0. Agent A proves its own permission bitmask via AgentPolicy (operator-signed EdDSA credential + cumulative-bit encoding). This root proof anchors the delegation chain — B and C can only narrow from A's proven bitmask. The selective-disclosure scope commitment output becomes the delegatorScopeCommitment input for the first Delegation proof.

Provides dual LeanIMT trees (agentTree) with 30-entry root history buffers and delegation nullifier replay protection. The on-chain chain-state tracking and hop-count enforcement (max 3) map directly to the Theseus multi-agent scenario. Would be redeployed or adapted on Theseus L1 as the authoritative delegation state anchor.

The deployed PLONK verifier at 0x285D2E315AdD9c82337d8a4BDF567AB3a9215B93 on Base Sepolia validates Delegation circuit proofs. A copy must be deployed on Theseus L1 for on-chain verification. No circuit changes required for the verifier itself — it is deterministic from the .zkey.
Verifies AgentPolicy proofs for the root-credential step (Agent A's initial capability claim). Must be redeployed on Theseus L1 alongside DelegationPlonkVerifier.

new_components_needed.description: AgentRootedDelegation circuit variant: current Delegation.circom assumes a human identity commitment at the trust root (humanNullifier binds chain). Theseus use case is fully agent-rooted (no human at root). Need to replace the human-leaf anchor with an operator-signed agent credential commitment as chain root, while preserving all scope-monotonicity and expiry-narrowing constraints. This is a structural change to the delegator input signals — not a rewrite, but non-trivial given the CIP-1 Merkle inclusion proof wiring.

TheseusRegistryAdapter: deploy PlonkVerifier + DelegationPlonkVerifier on Theseus L1 and a stripped-down IdentityRegistry that omits the humanTree and focuses on agentTree + delegation chain state. Must implement Theseus's native tx format if it diverges from EVM. If Theseus is EVM-compatible, this is a straightforward re-deploy of existing Hardhat artifacts with constructor param changes.

Complete the delegate() stub (currently v0.3 placeholder in @bolyra/sdk). Must support agent-as-root chains: buildDelegationProof(rootAgentCredential, delegateeAgentPubKey, narrowedPermissions, narrowedExpiry) → DelegationProof, and chainDelegation(existingChain, nextDelegatee, ...) for multi-hop. Must enforce ScopeEscalationError client-side before proof generation to fail fast.

@bolyra/theseus adapter: thin wrapper that maps Theseus agent identity format (however Theseus represents agents natively) to Bolyra AgentCredential, submits proofs to TheseusRegistryAdapter, and surfaces TheseusChainVerificationResult. Analogous to @bolyra/openclaw but targeting Theseus's agent-native RPC surface.

Theseus-specific conformance test vectors extending test-vectors.json: agent-rooted 3-hop chains, attempted privilege escalation (must fail), expiry narrowing boundary cases, and nullifier replay attempts. Needed to prove circuit correctness for the modified AgentRootedDelegation before any Theseus deployment.

theseus_integration_surface: Three-layer surface: (1) On-chain — deploy PlonkVerifier + DelegationPlonkVerifier + TheseusRegistryAdapter contracts on Theseus L1; Theseus agent contracts call verifyDelegationChain(proofs[], hopCount) which calls DelegationPlonkVerifier.verifyProof() for each hop and enforces the chain-state invariants. (2) SDK bridge — @bolyra/theseus adapter wraps the completed delegate() flow, translates Theseus agent identity format to AgentCredential, and submits proofs to the registry. Agent operators run proveHandshake-equivalent locally (Node or rapidsnark) and post proofs on-chain. (3) Off-chain verification — for Theseus dApps that want to gate API calls rather than on-chain actions, verifyHandshake() can run entirely off-chain against the agentTree root snapshot, with the Theseus registry serving as root-of-truth via a read RPC call.

Covers: AgentPolicy as chain root (hop 0), Delegation circuit per hop, IdentityRegistry as delegation state anchor, 8-bit scope monotonicity guarantee. Written for Sunday call — frames the integration before a single line of code is written.

Agent A gets FINANCIAL_MEDIUM (bit 3), delegates to B with READ_DATA+WRITE_DATA only, B delegates to C with READ_DATA only. Script prints proof hashes + on-chain verifyProof results. This is what gets shown on Sunday — no new circuit work needed, uses deployed 0x285D2E315AdD9c82337d8a4BDF567AB3a9215B93.

All scope-monotonicity constraints (delegateeScope ⊆ delegatorScope), expiry narrowing, max-3-hop counter, and identity-bound scope commitment chain linking are preserved verbatim. Exposes: registerAgent(agentCredentialHash), submitDelegation(proof, publicSignals), verifyChain(chainId, claimedScope) → bool. No human tree, no HumanUniqueness verifier. Designed for EVM-compatible Theseus — fallback path if Theseus is non-EVM is a JSON-RPC adapter stub.

Includes verify step that calls verifyProof with a known-good test proof to confirm chain compatibility. Blocked on Theseus providing testnet RPC + faucet.
